title: STANDING (re-confirmed): lume's headline 'ceremony pays for itself' claim has no instrumentation — no reject-rate, gate-friction, or ceremony-cost measurement exists anywhere in the engine. The protocol itself (lines 922-930, line 928) names this 'lume's single biggest unproven claim / the headline open question'. This is the UNADOPTED value-signal proposal from reviews 2026-06-11-01 and -03, surfaced per protocol line 1217 which prescribes reporting each UNADOPTED item as a standing finding.
context: reviews/2026-06-11-04: missed because Not missed — deliberately deferred. It is re-surfaced here (rather than silently dropped or re-proposed as new) because the prior-review follow-up loop and protocol line 1217 require standing UNADOPTED items to be re-affirmed with fresh evidence each cycle. Fresh evidence: grep across plugin/ confirms still no counters/timers/metrics (only prose prompt strings and store.py's ID-sequence _counter).; proposed: Re-affirm STANDING and convert to action this cycle via the proposed value-signal workstream (above), which derives the signal deterministically from already-persisted state. Until then, keep README/docs value claims qualitative and unembellished rather than asserting an unmeasured payoff.
